topics.title: AI Announcements
topics.executive_summary: Microsoft announced its own generative AI models at Build 2026, including MAI-Code-1-Flash for code generation and MAI-Thinking-1 for reasoning, signaling a strategic push to compete directly with OpenAI and reduce developer reliance on external models.

stories.headline: Microsoft unveils homegrown AI models at Build conference
stories.body: Microsoft announced MAI-Code-1-Flash, a code generation model, and MAI-Thinking-1, a reasoning model, at its Build developer conference in San Francisco. The move represents a shift toward competing directly with OpenAI and Anthropic while lowering costs for developers using Azure.

topics.title: Startups
topics.executive_summary: No major startup funding news was published in the last 24 hours that could be confirmed through search results, suggesting a quiet day in the startup sector.

topics.executive_summary: No major AI infrastructure news was published in the last 24 hours that could be confirmed through search results, suggesting a quiet day in the sector.

topics.title: Deep Tech
topics.executive_summary: Recent breakthroughs include a low-temperature hydrogen production method using perovskite catalysts, a quantum chip that encodes information using light's "valley" degree of freedom, and NASA's X-59 jet preparing for its first supersonic flight test.

stories.headline: Perovskite catalyst makes hydrogen from water at lower temperatures
stories.body: University of Birmingham researchers developed a perovskite-based catalyst that splits water into hydrogen at much lower temperatures than existing technologies, potentially enabling factories and renewable energy sites to convert waste heat into clean fuel.

stories.headline: Quantum chip controls light via valley degree of freedom
stories.body: Scientists created a chip that generates, steers, and reads light-based information using atomically thin materials and nanoscale structures, encoding data via the quantum property of light called the valley degree of freedom.

stories.headline: NASA X-59 jet prepares to break sound barrier
stories.body: After successful test flights approaching near-supersonic speeds, NASA's experimental X-59 aircraft is preparing to cross Mach 1 and eventually reach Mach 1.6 at 60,000 feet, designed to replace the thunderous sonic boom with a quieter alternative.
